title: Syntax of Meta Characters
text: Syntax of Meta Characters In keyboard translation tables, the characters in meta character sets can be mapped to a keystroke or combination of keystrokes. { FontNumber . } Character Arguments FontNumber —optional; if omitted, the default is Font 0. Character —literal character or its decimal value, for example B or ^66 . For example, the positions 1.A and 1.^065 are equivalent. Description In keyboard translation tables, the characters in meta character sets can be mapped to a keystroke or combination of keystrokes. For example, position 1.v usually contains the ö character. To map the keystroke combination PF1 v on the VT100 keyboard to this character, you might use the following line in a VT100 keyboard definition: ^ESC0Po 1.v Copy However, this is not always obvious for the person using the keyboard. If you do provide the facility to map keystrokes to font characters, consider printing a keyboard layout chart for the end user, or provide help via the keyboard help mechanism. Related Links Meta Character Sets Meta character sets are used for translating installable character sets and defining translation tables. In earlier versions of Uniface, meta character sets were used as internal character sets instead of Unicode. Defining Keyboard Help Text The keyboard help function (^KEY_HELP) looks for its text in a global message which has the same name as the current value of $KEYBOARD in the current library and language.
